nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; shallow protected lagoons and reef flats, typically buried in sand or rubble with only the head exposed. Occasionally swims between burrows. Depth range 1-8 m.

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and crevices

diet.notes: Piscivorous and crustacean-feeding moray. Newly introduced specimens often accept only live foods (ghost shrimp, small feeder fish) initially. Most can be transitioned over time to frozen krill, freeze-dried krill, strips of fresh marine fish, raw shrimp chunks, and squid offered on a feeding stick. Pieces should not be much larger than the eel's head. Feed every 2-3 days; targeted tong-feeding is generally advised to prevent tankmates from stealing food.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in its mouth
overly aggressive tankmates that may harass the ribbon-like tail

summary: Slender Indo-Pacific moray (~80-103 cm) with pale white-to-tan body, dark facial spots, and long protruding nostrils. Generally considered an advanced-care fish; tightly-sealed lid and patient feeding are essential.
careNotes:
The Ghost Eel (Pseudechidna brummeri), also widely sold as the White Ribbon Eel or Ghost Ribbon Eel, is a slender Indo-Pacific moray reaching about 100 cm. Body coloration is typically pale white to cream with tan, yellow, and dark markings; the head bears distinctive dark spots and elongated tubular nostrils. Older specimens have been reported to darken to light brown.

Generally advised for advanced aquarists. While LiveAquaria rates the species as moderate, the combination of feeding hurdles (many specimens initially refuse prepared foods and require live ghost shrimp), high escape risk, and adult size make it a poor choice for beginners.

A tightly-sealed lid or canopy is essential — this species has been known to escape through very small openings, and escape-mortality is a common cause of loss. Provide a deep sand bed and PVC tube or rockwork retreats; the eel spends most daylight hours partially buried or hidden, emerging mainly to feed or at night (largely cryptic/nocturnal).

Temperature reports diverge between hobby retailers and FishBase: LiveAquaria advises ~22-26°C while FishBase records a wild mean of ~28.5°C (range 26.4-29.3°C). The values written here (~24-27.5°C) reflect the averaged middle of those ranges; aquarists keeping wild-caught specimens may want to lean toward the warmer end.

Minimum tank: LiveAquaria advises 50 US gal (~190 L) but multiple sources note larger tanks are preferable for this 100-cm bottom-dwelling eel. ~250 L with a long footprint (120 cm+) is generally advised as a more realistic minimum given the body length and need for swimming/hiding space.

Reef compatibility is "with caution" — generally safe with soft corals, polyps, mushrooms, and filter feeders but will consume small ornamental shrimp and small fish.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been documented in home aquaria. The species is gonochoristic (separate sexes, unlike the protandrous Ribbon Eel). The larval phase is presumed pelagic and is not raised in captivity.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Designated primary; product page provided care level, water parameters, diet notes, temperament, reef safety, and color description.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Muraenidae, Anguilliformes), max length (103 cm TL), wild temperature range (26.4-29.3°C, mean 28.5°C), distribution (Indo-Pacific, W. Indian Ocean to Samoa, north to Ryukyu Islands), depth (1-8 m), and habitat. Note temperature spread vs LiveAquaria; averaged in final values.

additional.name: STL Marine Solutions
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (3'4" / ~101 cm), tank size (50 gal minimum), temperament (semi-aggressive), reef compatibility (with caution), and diet/feeding behaviour. Designated secondary (theaquariumwiki.com) returned HTTP 403 Access Denied; STL Marine Solutions used as replacement secondary.

additional.name: Live Gem Factory + Frags 2 Fishes (via search aggregation)
additional.notes: Used to triangulate lifespan (reported up to ~40 years in some sources; conservative range 10-20 years used here pending firmer data) and tank-size recommendations (some sources cite 75 gal minimum, biasing the chosen 250 L).

fish.breedingNotes: Gonochoristic species; not bred in home aquaria. Pelagic larvae presumed but not reared.
